name: Roasted Vegetable Stock
recipeIngredient: white part thinly sliced, plus 1 cup chopped inner greens
recipeInstructions:
Deborah Madison writes that, unlike meat stock, vegetable stock doesn't benefit from hours of cooking. After about 40 minutes of simmering, the vegetables have yielded all of their flavor.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
In a large bowl toss together vegetables, garlic and olive oil. Spread mixture evenly on a large baking sheet. Roast in preheated oven, stirring every 10 minutes, until browned, about 40 minutes.
Transfer roasted vegetables to a large soup pot and add remaining ingredients except for soy sauce. Bring to boil. Reduce heat to low, cover pot and simmer 40 minutes. Strain stock, pressing out as much liquid as possible from the vegetables. Add soy sauce for additional flavor, if desired. Use stock immediately or cover and refrigerate. Makes 6 cups.
Each serving contains approximately 1 fat exchange; 40 calories, 4 grams fat (including 1 gram saturated fat), 780 milligrams sodium and 7 milligrams calcium.
